Package io.forja.components.layout.fxSection

Class FxSection

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.layout.Pane
javafx.scene.layout.VBox
io.forja.components.layout.fxSection.FxSection
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget
public class FxSection extends javafx.scene.layout.VBox
A labeled content section composed of a title, optional leading separator, and arbitrary content children.

FxSection extends VBox and renders, in order:

  1. An FxSeparator (SeparatorVariant#DEFAULT) when setSeparator(true) is enabled.
  2. An FxLabel (LabelVariant.SUBHEADING) carrying the title.
  3. The user-supplied content nodes, in the order they were added.

The preferred way to construct an FxSection is via the builder:

     
      FxSection section = FxSection.builder()
          .title("Buttons")
          .separator(true)
          .content(primaryRow, secondaryRow)
          .gap(SpacingSize.MD)
          .build();
See Also:
  • FxLabel
  • FxSeparator
  • FxSection.Builder

      Sets the gap token between separator, title, and content children.

    • getContent

      public javafx.collections.ObservableList<javafx.scene.Node> getContent()
      Returns the mutable content list. Mutating triggers a rebuild.

      Returns the internal title label — for advanced styling.
